canonical_definition: A systematic and unfair tendency of a model to generate disproportionately positive or negative sentiments towards specific countries or regions, or to unfairly center or erase certain nations in its generations.
papers: Aspect-Oriented Summarization for Psychiatric Short-Term Readmission Prediction

definitions.text: A systematic and unfair tendency of a model to generate disproportionately positive or negative sentiments towards specific countries or regions, or to unfairly center or erase certain nations in its generations.

relationships.evidence: In our study, we consider a model as biased if its generations are representationally harmful (as per Blodgett et al., 2020) both in terms of how nations are described and represented as well as when nations are represented. (Section 1)

characterization: A systematic and unfair tendency of a model to generate disproportionately positive or negative sentiments towards specific countries or regions, or to unfairly center or erase certain nations in its generations.

emnlp25: Aspect-Oriented Summarization for Psychiatric Short-Term Readmission Prediction
